samples.xml_description:
<module id="fsm">
    <description>Detects a specific sequence of bits (10011) in a stream of input signals. Outputs MATCH = 1 when the sequence is detected.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="IN">Input signal to be detected.</input>
        <input id="CLK">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="RST">Reset signal.</input>
        <output id="MATCH">Output signal indicating whether the sequence has been detected.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state id="S0">Initial state.</state>
        <state id="S1">State after detecting '1'.</state>
        <state id="S2">State after detecting '10'.</state>
        <state id="S3">State after detecting '100'.</state>
        <state id="S4">State after detecting '1001'.</state>
        <state id="S5">State after detecting '10011'.</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="current_state" width_description="[2:0]">Current state register.</reg>
        <reg id="next_state" width_description="[2:0]">Next state register.</reg>
        <always @(posedge CLK or negedge RST)>
            if (!RST)
                current_state <= S0;
            else
                current_state <= next_state;
        </always>
    </logic_description>
    <logic_description type="combinational_logic">
        <wire id="match_wire">Match signal wire.</wire>
        <assign match_wire = (current_state == S5 && IN == 1) ? 1 : 0;
        <assign MATCH = match_wire;
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        // State transition logic
        always @(*) begin
            case(current_state)
                S0: begin
                    if(IN == 1)
                        next_state = S1;
                    else
                        next_state = S0;
                end
                
                S1: begin
                    if(IN == 0)
                        next_state = S2;
                    else
                        next_state = S1;
                end
                
                S2: begin
                    if(IN == 0)
                        next_state = S3;
                    else
                        next_state = S1;
                end
                
                S3: begin
                    if(IN == 1)
                        next_state = S4;
                    else
                        next_state = S0;
                end
                
                S4: begin
                    if(IN == 1)
                        next_state = S5;
                    else
                        next_state = S0;
                end
                
                default: next_state = S0;
            endcase
        end
        
        // Match logic
        always @(*) begin
            if(match_wire)
                MATCH = 1;
            else
                MATCH = 0;
        end
    </implementation>
</module>
